name: Bollinger Midline Vortex Trend Resumption
edge_hypothesis: The Bollinger Midline (BBM_20_2.0 = SMA_20) is the statistical center of the last 20 bars' price distribution — the fair value anchor for mean-reversion. It has NEVER been used as an entry trigger despite Bollinger Bands being available since inception. All prior BB strategies used the outer bands for breakouts (DEAD — bollinger_breakout template failed). The midline is a fundamentally different signal: it identifies when price RECLAIMS its statistical center after a pullback, not when price breaks to extremes. Vortex is the #1 overall family (Vortex Transition v3a = PF 2.034, champion). VTXP_14 > VTXM_14 confirms positive rotational momentum. The combination: price crossing back above its 20-bar statistical mean (BBM) while rotational momentum is positive (Vortex) = trend resumption from the center of gravity. This is structurally different from Vortex Transition specs (which use Vortex CROSSOVER as the primary trigger) — here Vortex is a FILTER confirming direction, while BBM reclaim is the timing signal. Ranging regimes should benefit from BBM oscillation; transitional regimes should benefit from Vortex's direction detection.

variants.name: bbm_vortex_resume_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core variant. Price reclaims Bollinger midline (statistical mean) while Vortex confirms positive rotational momentum. 8:1 R:R sweet spot, 1.5 ATR stop. Novel entry mechanism: midline reclaim + direction filter.

components.notes: Bollinger Midline (SMA_20) — FIRST EVER midline-as-entry test. Statistical center of price distribution, not outer band breakout.
Vortex positive > negative = rotational momentum confirming direction. Vortex is #1 family (PF 2.034).
ATR-based position sizing and 8:1 R:R framework.

variants.name: bbm_vortex_resume_v2_10to1
variants.description: Extended TP variant. 10:1 R:R (15x ATR TP). Midline entries start from the statistical center — if the trend is genuine and Vortex confirms, there should be significant runway above the mean. Tests whether midline entries support extended captures better than extreme entries.

components.notes: Bollinger Midline — statistical center entry for extended captures.
Vortex direction filter with wider TP target.
Extended 15x ATR TP for trend continuation from mean.

variants.name: bbm_vortex_resume_v3_tight_stop_8to1
variants.description: Tight stop variant. 1.0 ATR stop + 8.0 ATR TP (8:1 R:R maintained). Thesis: BBM entries are AT the statistical mean — price should move decisively if the signal is valid. Tight stop tests this conviction. NOTE: tight stops with EMA200 failed (v3 DD=40%), but BBM is a shorter-term mean where whipsaw may be less severe on 4h.

components.notes: Bollinger Midline — tight stop conviction test at statistical mean.
Vortex direction filter. Tight stop tests decisive mean-reclaim thesis.
Tight 1.0x stop / 8x TP — conviction-based DD control.

advisory_context.novel_element: FIRST EVER Bollinger Midline entry test — statistical mean reclaim (NOT outer band breakout) + champion Vortex direction filter

indicator_roles.baseline: BBM_20_2.0 — Bollinger Midline (SMA_20), statistical center of price distribution, never tested as entry trigger
indicator_roles.confirmation: VTXP_14 > VTXM_14 — Vortex positive momentum, #1 family overall (PF 2.034)
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — position sizing and R:R framework
